profile_basis: Ready [perf] bead frankenjax-17h7c, filed from the fj-interpreters pe_baseline profile after pass110; scan_sub_jaxpr_add_emit_128 was the next profile-backed interpreter hotspot.
lever: exact scalar-I64 carry plus scalar/vector-I64 xs add-emit scan direct evaluator before the generic functional scan loop
baseline_command: RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch-frankenjax-17h7c-scan-baseline cargo bench -p fj-interpreters --bench pe_baseline -- eval/scan_sub_jaxpr_add_emit_128 --sample-size 40 --measurement-time 2 --warm-up-time 1 --noplot
rebench_command: RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch-frankenjax-17h7c-scan-rebench cargo bench -p fj-interpreters --bench pe_baseline -- eval/scan_sub_jaxpr_add_emit_128 --sample-size 40 --measurement-time 2 --warm-up-time 1 --noplot
confirmation_rebench_command: RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch-frankenjax-17h7c-scan-confirm cargo bench -p fj-interpreters --bench pe_baseline -- eval/scan_sub_jaxpr_add_emit_128 --sample-size 40 --measurement-time 2 --warm-up-time 1 --noplot
isomorphism_proof: The direct path runs after the existing scan arity, const count, carry count, xs count, output arity, scan length, and zero-length-output validations, so those error-ordering surfaces are preserved.
The guard accepts only no-constvar/no-effect scan bodies with exactly two invars, two outvars, and exactly two Primitive::Add equations: carry + x -> carry_out, then carry_out + I64(0) -> y_out.

The scan equation itself must have no effects and either no params or exactly reverse=true; all other params and all body params/effects/sub_jaxprs miss the guard and fall back to the generic scan loop.
Accepted values are scalar I64 carry plus scalar I64 xs for length 1 or rank-1 I64 tensor xs; non-I64, malformed literal-backed tensors, non-rank-1 tensors, and multi-carry scans fall back.

Forward and reverse output ordering is preserved: forward writes ys in increasing input order, while reverse iterates indices in reverse and writes each accumulated y back to the original index, matching the generic per_y reverse.
Integer arithmetic uses i64::wrapping_add, matching Primitive::Add I64 scalar behavior. There is no floating-point, RNG, tie-breaking, or side-effect behavior in the accepted fragment.
validation: RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch-frankenjax-17h7c-scan-tests cargo test -p fj-interpreters scan --lib -- --nocapture
RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch-frankenjax-17h7c-lib-tests cargo test -p fj-interpreters --lib
RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch-frankenjax-17h7c-check cargo check -p fj-interpreters --all-targets
RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch-frankenjax-17h7c-clippy cargo clippy -p fj-interpreters --all-targets -- -D warnings
